mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from a restrictive level, equity discount rates decline and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 prices rise over the next month. This signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips positive only when CPIAUCSL is accelerating, indicating the rate-cut channel is not the driver.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, which should push S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month as earnings expectations are cut.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if PAYEMS remains stable while UNRATE rises and returns are still positive, implying labor weakness is not the causal channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in RSAFS indicates improving nominal sales and top-line momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through better revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS trend periods do not outperform negative trend periods on 20-day SPX returns over rolling 3-year windows, or if the effect is absent after controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to flat PAYEMS signals productivity-led output gains and better margins, which should lift S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The mechanism fails when output gains are driven by inventory noise rather than sustained industrial production growth.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not higher when INDPRO rises faster than PAYEMS over the prior quarter, or if the relationship disappears in windows where INDPRO is revised materially.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and RSAFS identifies broad domestic demand contraction in housing and retail, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical sectors reprice lower.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls month-over-month and RSAFS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint HOUST-RSAFS declines are followed by non-negative median 5-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect and the joint condition adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, FEDFUNDS above neutral and rising CPIAUCSL pressure real rates higher, which compresses valuation multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and rising CPIAUCSL do not deliver negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal turns positive in the same tightening regime.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS growth is still positive while UNRATE is flat or falling, the labor market is absorbing demand shocks and supporting consumption, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS together with non-rising UNRATE does not improve 20-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the effect vanishes once UNRATE is included alone.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-led downturns, a sustained drop in HOUST signals weakening durable-goods demand and credit conditions, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The signal is weakest when RSAFS is still accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month housing starts declines do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent specifically when RSAFS is not deteriorating, indicating housing is not the main channel.
